channel.title: John Wyles dot github dot io
channel.description: Recent content on John Wyles dot github dot io

item.title: Setting up Kubernetes on a Raspberry Pi cluster using Raspian
item.description: NOTE: This guide was based on a gist by GitHub user alexellis for setting up Kubernetes on Raspbian as well as a gist getting started with Raspian for each node which I have made some minor additions to in this post. Assumptions You have more than 1 Raspberry Pi with at least an 8GB SD card for each You want all of your Raspberry Pi’s to have a static IP You desire all of your networking and orchestration over WIFI rather than Ethernet For troubleshooting that you have access to your routers administrative panel to see attached devices and their IP address For further troubleshooting you may require Ethernet access for diagnosis and further setup Steps Download the latest Raspbian Lite image from here (as of writing this was: 2018-11-13 Raspbian Stretch Lite ): Raspbian Images If you are on a Mac use a software like balena Etcher to flash the image to only one of the Raspberry Pi SD cards that will be in your cluster.

item.title: Setting up Kubernetes on a Raspberry Pi cluster using Hypriot
item.description: This guide uses a Hypriot blog entry as a massive starting point but goes further to show the steps I took in order to get my kubernetes cluster of three Raspberry Pi computers running. This guide addresses much of the troubleshooting done to fill the gaps and/or “bugs” on this blog entry and applying it to my setup. Assumptions You have more than 1 Raspberry Pi with at least an 8GB SD card for each You want all of your Raspberry Pi’s to have a static IP You desire all of your networking and orchestration over WIFI rather than Ethernet For troubleshooting that you have access to your routers administrative panel to see attached devices and their IP address For further troubleshooting you may require Ethernet access for diagnosis and further setup Steps Setup Download the latest Hypriot disk image to a new directory named Hypriot and note the version number (as of writing: 1.

item.title: My First Post
item.description: This is my first post using Hugo.
